title: Koyamacalia rufipilis
intro: Koyamacalia rufipilis, commonly known as Koyamacalia, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The health benefits of Koyamacalia include its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Koyamacalia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Koyamacalia is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in modern pharmaceutical formulations to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
